_comment_target: Action-conditioned legacy variant v2 (ActionConditionedTembAdapter) on the full 802-clip droid_1k train split, FSDP. v2 = render Q ← cross_attn → 8-d (joint+gripper) actions K/V → LayerNorm → zero-init Linear → ADD to temb_base → time_proj. Identity-at-init via zero-init out_proj weight+bias; LayerNorm-before-proj guarantees ∂L/∂out_proj.weight ≠ 0 from step 1 (no dual-zero deadlock, no gate needed). 30 epochs targeted. The legacy egowm 30-epoch run (run_20260507_052841) is the baseline this should beat.
_metadata_note: Filtered version of train_metadata_train.csv (802 rows) → 795 rows after dropping 7 clips with fewer than 82 mp4 frames (which the num_frames=81 loader rejects). The dropped scenes: 386, 90, 498, 615, 331, 373, 444.
_num_frames_note: 81 = Wan 2.1 I2V's max-supported temporal length. T_lat=21 latent frames, num_tokens=8505. Self-attn O(N²) is ~17.6× more expensive per step vs 17 frames, but FFN dominates DiT compute and scales linearly → wall-clock per-step is only ~1.4× slower (verified: 25s→35s on 6×A100). Full 30-epoch run estimate: ~39 hr. The dataset filter requires every video to have ≥82 mp4 frames; the 802-row train split needs auditing for short clips before launch (smallest in 10-vid subset was 83, just barely sufficient).
_cache_note: 81-frame cache; will MISS and rebuild from scratch (~30 min for 802 videos through the VAE on a single GPU). Cache file size ~30-40 GB on disk.
